records.ety: [F. ascendant, L. ascendens; p. pr. of ascendere.]

quotes: Sciences that were then in their highest ascendant.

defs: The horoscope, or that degree of the ecliptic which rises above the horizon at the moment of one's birth; supposed to have a commanding influence on a person's life and fortune.
notes: ☞ Hence the phrases To be in the ascendant, to have commanding power or influence, and Lord of the ascendant, one who has possession of such power or influence; as, to rule, for a while, lord of the ascendant.

defs: Superiority, or commanding influence; ascendency; as, one man has the ascendant over another.
quotes: Chievres had acquired over the mind of the young monarch the ascendant not only of a tutor, but of a parent.

defs: An ancestor, or one who precedes in genealogy or degrees of kindred; a relative in the ascending line; a progenitor; -- opposed to descendant.
